paper.title: Resolving the Young 2 Cygni Run-away Star into a Binary using iLocater

review.summary: This paper reports the serendipitous AO imaging discovery of a candidate companion to the young runaway star 2 Cygni using the iLocater SX acquisition camera at LBT, with follow-up Keck/NIRC2 imaging and Lili spectroscopy. The paper contains five internal LaTeX tables: a parameter summary of the 2 Cygni system, a literature reference summary, an observing log, deblended NIRC2 photometry for the A and B components, and astrometric measurements across epochs. One external resource is cited: the BT-Settl spectral model database used for temperature comparison.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summarizes the basic observational properties of the 2 Cygni system (coordinates, proper motion, parallax, and multi-band photometry) drawn from Gaia and literature sources.

source_refs.caption: Observational properties of the 2 Cygni system. Gaia Early Data Release 3 (DR3) is from the \cite{gaia_dr3_21}.
source_refs.label: tab:2cyg

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Name of the observed stellar property

columns.name: Reference
columns.meaning: Source catalog or literature reference for the value

internal_tables.evidence: Observational properties of the 2 Cygni system. Gaia Early Data Release 3 (DR3) is from the \cite{gaia_dr3_21}.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists select literature references that mention 2 Cygni, together with brief comments on their multiplicity claims.

columns.name: Reference
columns.meaning: Bibliographic reference to the catalog or study

columns.name: Comments
columns.meaning: Brief note on what the reference says about multiplicity or binary status

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Observing log summarizing the dates, instruments, filters, integration parameters, airmass, and seeing for each AO imaging or spectroscopic epoch.

source_refs.caption: Observing log for AO measurements of 2~Cygni. iLocater's custom imaging channel senses light within a narrow range of wavelengths ($\lambda=0.927-0.960 \; \mu$m). The number of frames ($N_{\rm frames}$), integration time per frame ($\Delta t_{\rm int}$), and total integration time ($\Delta t_{\rm tot}$) are shown for each data set. Seeing measurements ($\theta$) were made using the LBT DIMM system (within 25$^{\circ}$ of target) and Keck MASS/DIMM system.
source_refs.label: tab:log

columns.name: \Delta t_\rm tot [sec]
columns.meaning: Total integration time for the epoch

internal_tables.evidence: Observing log for AO measurements of 2~Cygni. iLocater's custom imaging channel senses light within a narrow range of wavelengths ($\lambda=0.927-0.960 \; \mu$m). The number of frames ($N_{\rm frames}$), integration time per frame ($\Delta t_{\rm int}$), and total integration time ($\Delta t_{\rm tot}$) are shown for each data set. Seeing measurements ($\theta$) were made using the LBT DIMM system (within 25$^{\circ}$ of target) and Keck MASS/DIMM system.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents deblended apparent and absolute near-infrared photometry for the resolved 2 Cygni A and B components from NIRC2 AO imaging.

source_refs.caption: Deblended photometry for the 2~Cygni components using NIRC2. $H$-band data is based on $H_{\rm cont}$ measurements and $K_s$-band data is based on $K_{\rm cont}$ measurements.
source_refs.label: tab:photometry

columns.name: Star
columns.meaning: Identifier of the binary component (A or B)

columns.name: H-K_s
columns.meaning: Color index between H and K_s bands

internal_tables.evidence: Deblended photometry for the 2~Cygni components using NIRC2. $H$-band data is based on $H_{\rm cont}$ measurements and $K_s$-band data is based on $K_{\rm cont}$ measurements.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Reports astrometric measurements of angular separation, position angle, and magnitude difference for 2 Cygni across multiple epochs and instruments.

source_refs.caption: 2~Cygni astrometry showing angular separation ($\rho$), position angle (PA) measured east of north, and magnitude difference in the iLocater filter. Uncertainty in angular separation for iLocater astrometry measurements is dominated by uncertainty in the plate scale; calibration of iLocater's north-east orientation with changing parallactic angle is on-going.
source_refs.label: tab:astrometry

columns.name: Instrument
columns.meaning: Telescope/instrument used for the astrometric measurement

columns.name: \rho [mas]
columns.meaning: Angular separation between the two components

columns.name: PA [^\circ]
columns.meaning: Position angle of the secondary relative to the primary, measured east of north

columns.name: \Delta m
columns.meaning: Magnitude difference between the two components

internal_tables.evidence: 2~Cygni astrometry showing angular separation ($\rho$), position angle (PA) measured east of north, and magnitude difference in the iLocater filter. Uncertainty in angular separation for iLocater astrometry measurements is dominated by uncertainty in the plate scale; calibration of iLocater's north-east orientation with changing parallactic angle is on-going.

external_resources.description: BT-Settl spectral model database (via the Spanish Virtual Observatory) used to compare theoretical atmospheric models with the observed near-infrared spectra of 2 Cygni A and B.

source_refs.context: For comparison, we over-plot BT-Settl spectral models\footnote{\url{http://svo2.cab.inta-csic.es/theory/newov2/}} for different effective temperatures assuming a surface gravity of $\log(g)=4.0$ \citep{allard_11}.

external_resources.evidence: For comparison, we over-plot BT-Settl spectral models\footnote{\url{http://svo2.cab.inta-csic.es/theory/newov2/}} for different effective temperatures assuming a surface gravity of $\log(g)=4.0$ \citep{allard_11}.
